---
title: "Malaria Risk Mapping in Malaysia (Demo)"
author: "Alfa Pradana"
toc: true
number-sections: true
syntax-highlighting: pygments
format:
  html: 
    html-math-method: katex
editor: source
---

## Load spatial data

```{r setup}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# In report.qmd or a new script — run interactively first
source("R/risk_index.R")

library(terra)
library(sf)
library(geodata)
library(tidyterra)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
```

### Admin boundaries

```{r load boundaries}
mys <- gadm("MYS", level = 1, path = "data/raw/")

# Climate layers (annual mean temp °C, annual precip mm)
tmean <- worldclim_country("MYS", var = "tavg", path = "data/raw/")
prec  <- worldclim_country("MYS", var = "prec",  path = "data/raw/")
```

### Annual mean temperature (mean of 12 monthly layers)

```{r compute ann}
tmean_ann <- mean(tmean)
prec_ann  <- sum(prec) / 12   # average monthly precipitation
```

```{r plot mean ann}
plot(tmean_ann, main = "Annual Mean Temperature (°C)")
```

```{r plot prec ann}
plot(prec_ann, main = "Average Monthly Precipitation (mm)")
```



## Apply risk functions

```{r load func}
temp_suit <- temp_suitability(tmean_ann)
rain_suit <- rain_suitability(prec_ann)
```

### Crop to Malaysia boundary

```{r crop to mys}
temp_suit <- mask(crop(temp_suit, mys), mys)
rain_suit <- mask(crop(rain_suit, mys), mys)
```

```{r plot temp suit}
plot(temp_suit, main = "Temperature Suitability")
```

```{r plot rain suit}
plot(rain_suit, main = "Rainfall Suitability")
```

### Compute composite risk index

```{r compute risk index}
risk <- compute_risk_index(temp_suit, rain_suit)
```

```{r plot map risk}
plot(risk, main = "Composite Malaria Risk Index")
```


## Load and join case data

```{r}
#| echo: false
# IF using real data:
# cases <- read.csv("data/raw/malaria_cases.csv")

# IF using simulated fallback (paste the fallback block from pre-session)
```

### Generate dummy data

```{r generate data}
set.seed(42)
malaria_sim <- data.frame(
  NAME_1   = c("Sabah","Sarawak","Kelantan","Pahang","Perak",
               "Terengganu","Kedah","Johor","Negeri Sembilan",
               "Melaka","Selangor","Pulau Pinang","Perlis",
               "Putrajaya","Kuala Lumpur","Labuan"),
  cases    = c(4820,2310,890,670,540,430,310,280,190,
               120,95,60,40,15,10,8),
  population = c(3900000,2800000,1900000,1700000,2500000,
                 1200000,2200000,3800000,1100000,
                 930000,6500000,1800000,250000,
                 100000,1800000,100000)
) |> dplyr::mutate(
  incidence = (cases / population) * 1000,
  risk_cat  = cut(incidence, breaks = c(0,0.1,0.5,1,5,Inf),
                  labels = c("Very Low","Low","Moderate","High","Very High"))
)

# Convert to sf for joining
mys_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(mys)
mys_sf <- dplyr::left_join(mys_sf, malaria_sim, by = "NAME_1")
```


## Visualise

### Climate risk map

```{r map 1}
p1 <- ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster(data = risk) +
  geom_sf(data = mys_sf, fill = NA, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1,
                       name = "Climate\nRisk Index",
                       na.value = "transparent") +
  labs(title = "Climate Suitability for Malaria Transmission",
       subtitle = "Temperature + Rainfall composite index",
       caption = "WorldClim v2.1 | Mordecai et al. 2013") +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
p1
```

### Reported case incidence choropleth

```{r map 2}
p2 <- ggplot(mys_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = incidence), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Reds", direction = 1,
                       name = "Incidence\n(per 1,000)",
                       na.value = "grey90") +
  labs(title = "Reported Malaria Incidence",
       subtitle = "Cases per 1,000 population",
       caption = "Source: WHO / MoH Malaysia") +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
p2
```

Plot side-by-side

```{r visualisation}
#| column: page
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 6
p1 + p2
```

Save image for sharing

```{r ggsave}
#| eval: false

ggsave("outputs/maps/malaria_risk_malaysia.png", 
       width = 14, 
       height = 6, 
       dpi = 200)
```
